PARAM.assertion: assert receiverObjectID.getInf() <= args[0] && receiverObjectID.getSup() >= args[0] && args[0] == null || receiverObjectID.getInf() <= args[0] && receiverObjectID.getSup() >= args[0];
PARAM.name: lower

PARAM.description: upper must be greater or equal to lower (may be Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY)

PARAM.name: upper

PARAM.description: the leaf nodes of the BSP tree must have a Boolean attribute representing the inside status of the corresponding cell
PARAM.name: tree
PARAM.content: The leaf nodes of the BSP tree must have a {@code Boolean} attribute representing the inside status of the corresponding cell (true for inside cells, false for outside cells).

PARAM.name: tolerance
PARAM.content: In order to avoid building too many small objects, it is recommended to use the predefined constants {@code Boolean.TRUE} and {@code Boolean.FALSE}

PARAM.name: tolerance
PARAM.content: tolerance below which points are considered identical.

lower must be lesser or equal to upper (may be Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY)
PARAM.name: lower
PARAM.content: lower bound of the interval, must be lesser or equal to

PARAM.description: upper must be greater or equal to lower (may be Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY)

PARAM.content: upper bound of the interval, must be greater or equal to

PARAM.description: the tree must be a BSPTree
PARAM.name: tree

PARAM.description: the boundary must be a collection
PARAM.name: boundary

RETURN.description: lowest value belonging to the instance. ({@code Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY} if the instance doesn't have any low bound, {@code Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY} if the instance is empty)
RETURN.assertion: assert methodResultID == (Double.isNaN(methodResultID) ? Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY : Double.isInfinite(methodResultID) ? Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY : receiverObjectID.getInf());
RETURN.content: lowest value belonging to the instance. ({@code Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY} if the instance doesn't have any low bound, {@code Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY} if the instance is empty)

RETURN.description: highest value belonging to the instance ({@code Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY} if the instance doesn't have any high bound, {@code Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY} if the instance is empty)
RETURN.content: highest value belonging to the instance ({@code Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY} if the instance doesn't have any high bound, {@code Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY} if the instance is empty)

RETURN.description: a new ordered list containing Interval elements
RETURN.assertion: assert methodResultID instanceof java.util.List<java.lang.Object> && receiverObjectID.asList().size() == 1 && (receiverObjectID.asList().get(0) instanceof java.util.List<java.lang.Integer> && receiverObjectID.asList().get(0).isEmpty()) || (receiverObjectID.asList().get(0) instanceof java.util.List<java.lang.Integer> && !receiverObjectID.asList().get(0).isEmpty());
RETURN.content: a new ordered list containing Interval elements
